title: smilax bona-nox var. senticosa
intro: Smilax bona-nox var. senticosa, also known as the Devil's Walking Stick or Black Brier, is a variety of the American Smilax plant, a species of flowering plant in the family Smilacaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Smilax bona-nox var. senticosa include relieving pain, reducing fever, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, and are often used to treat a variety of ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues.
